control_text: 1.1.1: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 1 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
rationale: Classic meta: solely governs documentation/awareness of Requirement 1 policies.

control_text: 2.1.1: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 2 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
rationale: Classic meta: solely governs documentation/awareness of Requirement 2 policies.

control_text: 3.1.1: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 3 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
rationale: Classic meta: solely governs documentation/awareness of Requirement 3 policies.

control_text: 6.1.1: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 6 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
rationale: Classic meta: solely governs documentation/awareness of Requirement 6 policies.

control_text: 1.1.2: Roles and responsibilities for performing activities in Requirement 1 are documented, assigned, and understood.
rationale: Meta: governs role documentation for Requirement 1, no specific security action.

control_text: 8.1.2: Roles and responsibilities for performing activities in Requirement 8 are documented, assigned, and understood.
rationale: Meta: governs role documentation for Requirement 8, no specific security action.

control_text: 10.1.1: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 10 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
rationale: Classic meta: solely governs documentation/awareness of Requirement 10 policies.

control_text: 8.3.1: All user access to system components for users and administrators is authenticated via at least one of the following authentication factors: Something you know, Something you have, Something you are.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes specific authentication factor requirements.

control_text: 1.2.1: Configuration standards are defined and implemented for all network security controls (NSCs).
rationale: Substantive: prescribes defining and implementing NSC configuration standards.

control_text: 3.4.1: Primary Account Number (PAN) is masked when displayed, so that only personnel with a legitimate business need can see more than the BIN and last four digits.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes specific data masking for PAN display.

control_text: 6.2.1: Bespoke and custom software is developed securely.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes secure software development practices.

control_text: 8.2.1: All users are assigned a unique ID before access to system components or cardholder data is allowed.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes unique user identification.

control_text: 12.1.1: An overall information security policy is established, published, maintained, and disseminated to all relevant personnel and to relevant vendors and business partners.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes establishing and publishing a specific policy.

control_text: 12.1.2: The information security policy clearly defines information security roles and responsibilities for all personnel.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes that the policy defines roles, not just documents OTHER requirements.

control_text: 12.5.1: An inventory of system components that are in scope for PCI DSS, including a description of function/use, is maintained and kept current.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes maintaining a specific inventory.

control_text: 3.5.1: PAN is secured wherever it is stored.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes securing stored PAN data.

control_text: 8.3.6: If passwords/passphrases are used as authentication factors, they meet minimum complexity: at least 12 characters containing both numeric and alphabetic characters.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes specific password complexity requirements.

control_text: 12.4.1: Responsibility is formally assigned for the protection of cardholder data and a PCI DSS compliance program.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes formally assigning responsibility to a specific role.

control_text: 8.2.2: Group, shared, or generic accounts, or other shared authentication credentials are only used when necessary on an exception basis.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes restrictions on shared/group accounts.

control_text: 1.4.1: NSCs are implemented between trusted and untrusted networks.
rationale: Substantive: prescribes implementing network security controls.
